releases.title: collectZ v3.16.7
releases.summary: This patch makes the top of the mobile navigation menu read as a compact utility row instead of a tall brand block.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed the visible mobile wordmark from the open navigation menu top row.
Kept the collectZ mark, release version, and close action available in a shorter row.
Replaced the shared icon-button styling on the mobile menu close action with a local compact button.
Kept desktop sidebar branding unchanged.
Tightened browser coverage so the mobile menu top row stays compact.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.6
releases.summary: This patch reduces the mobile navigation drawer header height so the drawer opens closer to the actual navigation choices.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Tightened the mobile drawer brand row spacing.
Reduced the mobile collectZ mark and wordmark sizing inside the open drawer.
Kept the desktop sidebar spacing unchanged.
The drawer action button now closes the mobile drawer when the drawer is open.
Browser coverage now protects the compact mobile drawer header height.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.5
releases.summary: This patch tightens the last visible Platform Runtime wording from the 3.16 boundary cleanup and updates the backlog to match the work already completed.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Activity links that point to platform Logs or Metrics now say `Open runtime` instead of `Open integrations`.
Workspace activity still points to `Open integrations` for workspace-owned provider setup.
The Platform vs Workspace backlog item now records completed 3.16 work and leaves only internal route cleanup or deeper override fixtures as follow-up candidates.
Source-level coverage protects the Platform Runtime activity action label.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.4
releases.summary: This patch moves backup/export readback into Workspace Settings while keeping Platform Settings clearly scoped to instance-wide backup/export.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added workspace-scoped portability status and export endpoints under `/api/spaces/:id/portability`.
Workspace Settings now shows `Workspace backup and portability` with scope readback for the selected workspace.
Workspace JSON/CSV exports reuse the existing redacted portability export behavior while filtering database rows to the selected workspace.
Platform Settings keeps its existing platform-wide export surface.
Portability status and export manifests now include explicit `scope` metadata.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.3
releases.summary: This patch makes Workspace Integrations explain whether each provider is using workspace-owned settings, collectZ defaults, or no usable configuration.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `integrationScope` readback to workspace integration responses.
Workspace Integrations now shows a compact source badge for each provider section.
Provider sections identify `Workspace`, `Default`, or `Not configured` beside the existing connection status.
The source readback uses meaningful overrides such as saved keys, custom URLs, provider usernames, client IDs, Plex sections, CWA URL, and Kavita base URL.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.2
releases.summary: This patch makes platform admins explicitly choose between workspace/library work and the platform control plane.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a Workspace/Platform mode switch to the sidebar for platform admins.
Workspace mode shows collection work, library tools, import, Help, Workspace settings/integrations, and workspace review tools.
Platform mode shows platform-only surfaces such as Platform Settings, Runtime, Platform Activity, All Workspaces, and All Members.
Merge Review remains under Workspace and is hidden from Platform mode.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.1
releases.summary: This patch finishes the visible Platform vs Workspace boundary cleanup by moving Merge Review out of the Platform nav group.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Changed Workspace into an expandable sidebar group when workspace-owned tools are available.
Moved `Merge Review` under Workspace.
Kept Platform focused on platform-level settings, runtime, activity, workspaces, and members.
Preserved the existing internal merge-review route ID and behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.0
releases.summary: This release clarifies the app boundary between workspace-owned collection work and platform-level setup.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Renamed the visible `Admin` nav group to `Platform`.
Renamed platform settings to `Platform Settings` with copy that explains the instance-wide scope.
Renamed workspace settings to `Workspace Settings` with copy that explains the selected-workspace scope.
Renamed workspace provider setup to `Workspace Integrations`.
Changed the hosted platform runtime surface to show platform runtime concerns instead of editable data-provider setup.
Dashboard provider health now points toward Workspace, where provider/data-sync setup belongs.

releases.title: collectZ v3.15.4
releases.summary: This patch adds a non-destructive restore rehearsal readback so admins can tell whether they have the pieces needed to practice a restore in a separate test stack.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `restore_rehearsal` to `GET /api/admin/settings/portability`.
Admin Settings now shows a Restore rehearsal card with database export, backup freshness, uploaded image binary, and restore dry-run checks.
The restore rehearsal response explicitly reports `destructive: false`.
Restore guidance now calls out rehearsal in a separate test stack.
The backup runbook now includes a restore rehearsal checklist.

releases.title: collectZ v3.15.3
releases.summary: This patch lets the backup and portability surface show whether an external scheduled backup job has reported a recent successful run.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added optional backup freshness readback to `GET /api/admin/settings/portability`.
Added a marker-file contract using `COLLECTZ_BACKUP_STATUS_PATH` and `COLLECTZ_BACKUP_FRESHNESS_HOURS`.
Admin Settings now shows a compact Backup freshness card next to database, images, and provider metadata.
Backup checks now include a freshness row with plain status detail.
The backup runbook documents the scheduled-backup marker JSON shape.
